skill.name: stitch-design
skill.description: Enhances Stitch design prompts, generates or edits UI screens through Stitch MCP, and documents project design systems in .stitch/DESIGN.md.
skill.summary: Create consistent Stitch UI designs from prompts and project design systems.
skill.author: google-labs-code

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown code spans, hex colors, IDs, and design terminology. The confirmed concerns are legitimate but elevated-risk behavior: Stitch MCP network access, generated asset downloads, shell-based curl guidance, and local writes under .stitch.

medium_findings.title: Remote Asset Download via Shell Command
medium_findings.description: The workflow downloads HTML and screenshot URLs from Stitch output into .stitch/designs and suggests curl -o through run_command. This supports the design workflow, but it can persist untrusted remote HTML or overwrite local design artifacts if URLs and filenames are not reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The asset download behavior is explicit, and workflows/text-to-design.md line 37 directly suggests curl -o. The behavior is probably legitimate for Stitch, so the confidence is high for elevated risk but not for malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Read and Write Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Read and Write tools and writes .stitch/DESIGN.md plus generated assets. This is necessary for design-system documentation, but project content from Stitch should be treated as untrusted before it influences local file writes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access and write paths are clearly documented. There is no evidence of arbitrary path construction or destructive writes, so the concern remains moderate.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography and Sensitive Keyword Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto, Windows SAM, and C2 alerts are caused by design text, hex color values, project IDs, screen IDs, and the phrase DESIGN SYSTEM. No cryptographic operations, credential access, or command-and-control behavior were found in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows ordinary UI design tokens and documentation examples. The suspicious strings are not used as executable code or security-sensitive data.

low_findings.title: Markdown Relative Links Mistaken for Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts point to Markdown links such as ../SKILL.md and ../references paths. These are documentation links, not filesystem reads or writes driven by user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are static Markdown references. No runtime path joining, file copy, or user-controlled path usage appears there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell-Based Download of MCP-Provided URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow recommends curl -o for downloading generated assets. Implementations should validate URLs, keep writes inside .stitch/designs, and avoid executing downloaded HTML.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell download recommendation is explicit. The risk depends on the MCP output and local filename handling, so it is a warning rather than a confirmed exploit.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose visual consistency when prompts are vague or screens are generated one at a time. This skill turns rough UI requests into structured Stitch prompts and keeps project design rules documented.
actual_capabilities: Enhances rough UI ideas with structured design language for Stitch generation.
Routes requests to text-to-design, edit-design, or design-system documentation workflows.
Uses Stitch MCP project and screen tools to generate or edit UI screens.
Creates .stitch/DESIGN.md files that summarize colors, typography, layout, and component rules.
Downloads generated HTML and screenshots into the project .stitch/designs directory.
Surfaces Stitch output descriptions and suggestions after tool calls.
limitations: Requires access to a configured Stitch MCP server and accessible Stitch projects.
Generated visuals depend on Stitch output quality and the clarity of the prompt.
It does not implement production frontend code by itself.
Downloaded HTML and screenshots should be reviewed before reuse in sensitive projects.

use_cases.title: Generate a New Product Screen
use_cases.description: Turn a short page idea into a structured Stitch prompt and generate a high-fidelity screen.

use_cases.title: Document an Existing Visual System
use_cases.description: Analyze representative Stitch screens and create a reusable .stitch/DESIGN.md reference.

use_cases.title: Refine Screens Before Implementation
use_cases.description: Apply targeted edits to generated screens before handing assets to frontend engineers.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new design from a short description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a desktop landing page for a project management app. Use a clean professional style, a hero section, feature cards, and a clear primary action.

prompt_templates.title: Match an Existing Design System
prompt_templates.scenario: Use project design rules when generating a new screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a mobile onboarding screen for this Stitch project. Use the existing .stitch/DESIGN.md colors, typography, button shape, and spacing rules.

prompt_templates.title: Edit a Generated Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Target a specific screen and request controlled changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the current dashboard screen. Make the navigation more compact, increase table readability, and keep the existing palette and typography unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Project Design Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for documenting a Stitch project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the main dashboard and landing page in this Stitch project. Create or update .stitch/DESIGN.md with palette roles, typography rules, layout principles, and component styling guidance.

output_examples.input: Design a premium landing page for a wellness app using blue and green.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a structured Stitch prompt with a calm visual theme, named color roles, typography guidance, and a section-by-section page plan.

output_examples.input: Make this screen feel more enterprise-ready without changing the layout.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a targeted edit request that adjusts color contrast, button treatment, spacing, and surface styling while preserving the screen structure.

output_examples.input: Create a design system file for this Stitch project.
output_examples.output: The skill reviews selected screens and writes a design reference covering atmosphere, colors, typography, components, and layout rules.

best_practices: Provide the target platform, screen type, audience, and brand mood before generation.
Create .stitch/DESIGN.md early so future screens share the same visual language.
Review downloaded HTML and screenshots before using them as implementation references.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for vague changes such as make it better without naming the target area.
Do not regenerate an entire screen when a focused Stitch edit can solve the issue.
Do not treat generated HTML as production code without review and cleanup.

faq.question: What does this skill need to work?
faq.answer: It needs Stitch MCP access and a Stitch project that the agent can list or identify.

faq.question: Can it generate complete application code?
faq.answer: No. It generates and edits Stitch design screens, then saves design assets for review.

faq.question: Does it create a design system document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create .stitch/DESIGN.md from representative Stitch screens and project metadata.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing Stitch screen?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses targeted edit prompts with Stitch MCP when a project ID and screen ID are known.

faq.question: Why is the security risk marked medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses networked Stitch tools and downloads generated assets to local files. This is useful but needs review.

faq.question: Should downloaded assets be trusted automatically?
faq.answer: No. Review generated HTML and screenshots before using them in production or sensitive repositories.
